_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 1-4 Syncope (Revised 05/08/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan General Treatment Protocol Section 1-4: Syncope — MDHHS Approved 5/8/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: Syncope
points: Assess for mechanism of injury; if trauma sustained, refer to General Trauma-Treatment Protocol
Pediatric patients (< 14 years of age): utilize MI MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage; when unavailable, use pediatric dosing listed within protocol
Position patient: if third trimester pregnancy, position left lateral recumbent; supine for all other patients
Check blood glucose (may be MFR skill per Blood Glucose Testing-Procedure Protocol)
If altered mental status: perform stroke assessment and evaluate for stroke per Stroke/Suspected Stroke-Treatment Protocol
If altered mental status: refer to Adult or Pediatric Altered Mental Status-Treatment Protocol

For signs of dehydration or hypotension: administer NS or LR IV/IO fluid bolus (Specialist skill — refer to Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol); Adults: up to 1 liter; Pediatrics: up to 20 mL/kg

Hypotensive/dehydrated patients should receive additional IV/IO fluid boluses as indicated: Adults repeat to maximum 2 liters; Pediatrics repeat 20 mL/kg to maximum 40 mL/kg; monitor for pulmonary edema — if presents, stop fluids and contact Medical Control
Obtain 12-lead ECG (per MCA selection — may be BLS or Specialist procedure) per 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol; if ECG indicates cardiac event or dysrhythmia, refer to appropriate Cardiac Protocol
Contact Medical Control for additional IV fluids

mca_selections_required.description: May be designated as an MFR skill by MCA
May be designated as a BLS skill or Specialist (AEMT/Paramedic) skill

deterioration_flags: Hypotension — initiate IV/IO fluid bolus (Specialist skill); consider cardiac etiology and obtain 12-lead ECG
Altered mental status — perform stroke assessment; refer to Altered Mental Status protocol
Dysrhythmia on cardiac monitor — refer to appropriate Cardiac Protocol
Hypoglycemia on blood glucose check — refer to Altered Mental Status-Treatment Protocol
Recurrent syncope or syncope with exertion — consider cardiac etiology; ALS priority
scope_notes: Blood glucose testing: may be MFR skill per MCA selection
12-Lead ECG: scope per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
IV/IO access and fluid bolus: Specialist skill
Cardiac monitoring: Specialist skill unless MCA-approved for lower level
Stroke assessment: all levels — refer to Stroke protocol
